name: open-shortlink
description: Open-source URL shortener on Cloudflare Workers, with REST API and Remote MCP server.

OIDC_ISSUER.description: **Required unless using Cloudflare Access.** OpenID Connect issuer URL (e.g. `https://accounts.google.com`, your Auth0/Okta/Entra tenant, or a Keycloak realm). Register a new OAuth app at that IdP and set its redirect URI to `https://<this-worker>/oauth/callback`. See the [security policy](https://tied-inc.github.io/open-shortlink/guide/security).

OIDC_CLIENT_ID.description: Required with OIDC_ISSUER. The client_id your IdP issued when you registered this Worker as an OAuth application.

OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET.description: Required with OIDC_ISSUER. The confidential client secret. Store it as a Worker Secret, not a plain variable.

OIDC_ALLOWED_SUBS.description: Required with OIDC_ISSUER. Comma-separated allowlist of email addresses (or `sub` claims) permitted to sign in. Without this the worker refuses all upstream identities.

OIDC_SCOPES.description: Optional. Space-separated OAuth scopes requested from the upstream IdP. Defaults to `openid email profile`.

CF_ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN.description: **Alternative to OIDC.** Set this (plus CF_ACCESS_AUD and ACCESS_ALLOWED_EMAILS) when this Worker is fronted by a Cloudflare Access application. Example: `acme.cloudflareaccess.com`.

CF_ACCESS_AUD.description: Required with CF_ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN. The AUD tag of the Access application fronting this Worker.

ACCESS_ALLOWED_EMAILS.description: Required with CF_ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN. Comma-separated allowlist of email addresses permitted to sign in.

CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN.description: Optional. Comma-separated origin allowlist for CORS (e.g. `https://ui.example.com`). Leave empty or `*` to allow any origin.

PUBLIC_BASE_URL.description: Optional. Canonical origin returned in `shortUrl` responses. Set this once you bind a custom domain (e.g. `https://go.example.com`).
